message:
perf(search): optimize hit field filtering with branch-specific pipelines

Add FieldBudgets::has_any_limit() helper to cheaply detect whether any
content truncation is configured. Split the filtered_hits construction
into four specialized branches:

1. Minimal projection (source_path + line_number + agent): hand-built
   JSON objects, no serde serialization — unchanged fast path.
2. Passthrough all fields, no truncation: serialize hits once via
   serde_json::to_value without per-hit field filtering or truncation,
   avoiding unnecessary work when callers request all fields with no
   content limits.
3. Field filtering + truncation: apply both filter_hit_fields and
   apply_content_truncation — the previous default path.
4. Field filtering only, no truncation: skip the truncation pass when
   no content budgets are configured but specific fields are requested.

This eliminates redundant serialization and truncation overhead for the
common --fields=all and --fields=minimal cases.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
